title: A Thesis (Skripsi) with Quarto
text:
🌐 Versi Bahasa Indonesia: baca dalam Bahasa Indonesia
Quarto isn’t just for plain documents — it can produce a full thesis with a print layout exactly like a real skripsi: cover page, approval page, Chapters I–V, automatic numbering, lists of contents/tables/figures, and a bibliography. You write the content and analysis; Quarto handles the formatting.

title: A Thesis (Skripsi) with Quarto
section: The result: a true thesis layout
text:
The result: a true thesis layout
The example below is rendered from the thesis template (Central Java poverty case study) — its print layout matches a real skripsi:






Cover page — title, “SKRIPSI”, FEB Universitas Diponegoro, name & student ID







Numbered chapter + automatic Table 5.1 & coloured cross-references





Figures, maps, and statistical tests appear in thesis layout too — all computed from the data, never pasted by hand:



Figure 5.1 (map with OSM basemap) + classical-assumption tests, in thesis layout

title: A Thesis (Skripsi) with Quarto
text:
What Quarto handles automatically

Front matter: cover, approval page, abstract, preface.
Numbering: “Chapter I–V”, Table 5.1 / Figure 5.1, equations — with clickable cross-references.
Automatic lists: table of contents, list of tables, list of figures.
Citations & bibliography: APA (or other) styles, linked to Zotero/.bib.
Analysis results: descriptive tables, charts, maps, assumption tests, regression — straight from code + data (see the Data Analysis page).

title: A Thesis (Skripsi) with Quarto
text:
Thesis project structure
thesis/
├── _quarto.yml          # "book" project: chapter order, PDF/Word format
├── index.qmd            # title page / abstract
├── bab/
│   ├── 01-pendahuluan.qmd      # Chapter I (Introduction)
│   ├── 02-tinjauan-pustaka.qmd # Chapter II (Literature)
│   ├── 03-metode.qmd           # Chapter III (Methods)
│   ├── 04-hasil.qmd            # Chapter IV/V (Results & Discussion)
│   └── 05-penutup.qmd
├── data/                # your data (.csv, .geojson)
├── tex/preamble.tex     # LaTeX style (margins, Times font, chapter numbering)
└── referensi.bib        # references (export from Zotero)

title: A Thesis (Skripsi) with Quarto
text:
How to use it

Grab a ready template (R or Python) — see Downloads below.
Write each chapter in bab/*.qmd (plain Markdown + code blocks for analysis).
Swap in your data under data/.
Render → thesis PDF. (For Word: --to docx.)
Data/variables change? Re-render — tables, charts, maps, numbers, and the bibliography update automatically.







TipMatch your campus format



Margins, font (e.g. Times New Roman 12pt), 1.5 line spacing, and numbering live in tex/preamble.tex and _quarto.yml — set them once, applied across the whole thesis.

title: A Thesis (Skripsi) with Quarto
text:
Downloads & templates

📄 Download the example thesis (PDF) — 35 pages, rendered from the template.
📦 Download a ready-to-render template (ZIP): R template · Python template — complete with bab/, data/, tex/preamble.tex, referensi.bib, and defense slides.
🔎 Browse / clone the template source in this (public) workshop repo: https://github.com/firmanhadi21/quarto-workshop/tree/main/templates
📘 Full book “Quarto untuk Skripsi” (chapter-by-chapter guide; Indonesian): https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/







NoteUsing the ZIP




Download & unzip. 2. Open the folder in RStudio (click *.Rproj). 3. Replace data/ and write each chapter in bab/. 4. Hit Render → thesis PDF.

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text: 🌐 Versi Bahasa Indonesia: baca dalam Bahasa Indonesia

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
Agenda (~60 min)



Time
Activity




⏱️ 5′
0. Check your setup


⏱️ 10′
1. Your first document (HTML + PDF)


⏱️ 15′
2. Prose + code + data


⏱️ 10′
3. The reproducible payoff (change the data)


⏱️ 10′
4. Slides from the same source


⏱️ 10′
5. Q&A & next steps



Download the sample data: data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv (from the workshop repo). Put it in your working folder under a data/ subfolder.

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 0 · Check your setup (5′)
Create a new working folder, open it in RStudio, then in the Terminal:
quarto check
Look for (✓) for Quarto and LaTeX. (If using Python: also for Jupyter.)

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 1 · Your first document (10′)
Create hello.qmd:
---
title: "Hello Quarto"
format: html
---

## Welcome
A normal paragraph: **bold**, *italic*, `code`. A list:

- one
- two
Click Render (or quarto render hello.qmd). Then change format: html → format: pdf and render again. Same source, two outputs. 🎉

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 2 · Prose + code + data (15′)
The heart of Quarto: code runs at render time and its results flow into the document. Create analysis.qmd (pick R or Python):

RPython


---
title: "Central Java Poverty"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2)
df <- read.csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 <- df[df$tahun == 2023, ]
```

Mean poverty in 2023 = **`r round(mean(df23$kemiskinan), 2)`%**.

```{r}
df |> group_by(tahun) |> summarise(rata = mean(kemiskinan)) |>
  ggplot(aes(tahun, rata)) + geom_line() + geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Poverty (%)")
```


---
title: "Central Java Poverty"
format: html
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = pd.read_csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 = df[df["tahun"] == 2023]
print(f"Mean 2023: {df23['kemiskinan'].mean():.2f}%")
```

```{python}
df.groupby("tahun")["kemiskinan"].mean().plot(marker="o")
plt.xlabel("Year"); plt.ylabel("Poverty (%)"); plt.show()
```



Render. Notice: the inline number and the chart both come from the data — no copy-paste.

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 3 · The reproducible payoff (10′)
The “aha” moment:

Open data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv.
Change Kota Semarang 2023 from 4.58 → 24.58. Save.
Re-render analysis.qmd.

The mean and the chart change instantly. One data edit → the whole document follows.






ImportantMust know: quarto render, not just preview



The live preview (on localhost) does not watch data files. After editing data, run quarto render in the Terminal and open the result — or edit-and-save a .qmd to make the preview refresh. (This is the most common gotcha.)

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 4 · Slides from the same source (10′)
No rewriting needed. Edit the header of analysis.qmd to add two slide formats:
---
title: "Central Java Poverty"
format:
  revealjs: default     # HTML slides (screen/web)
  beamer:               # PDF slides (projector)
    aspectratio: 169
---
Then:
quarto render analysis.qmd --to revealjs   # -> analysis.html
quarto render analysis.qmd --to beamer     # -> analysis.pdf
Each ## heading becomes a slide; your chart appears on a slide — still from the same data.

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 5 · Done & next steps (10′)
You just: wrote a document, ran analysis inside it, proved reproducibility, and made slides — all from one source.
Keep going:

📘 Book “Quarto untuk Skripsi” (complete, Chapter I–V, ready templates; Indonesian) — https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/
📦 Thesis templates (R & Python) + defense slides — in the book’s repo.
📑 Quarto documentation — https://quarto.org/docs/guide/
🔖 Citations via Zotero — https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/citations.html

title: Skripsi dengan Quarto
text:
🌐 English version: read this in English
Quarto bukan hanya untuk dokumen biasa — ia bisa menghasilkan skripsi utuh dengan tata letak cetak persis seperti skripsi: sampul, halaman pengesahan, BAB I–V, penomoran otomatis, daftar isi/tabel/gambar, hingga daftar pustaka. Anda menulis isi dan analisis; Quarto mengurus format.

title: Skripsi dengan Quarto
text:
Hasilnya: tata letak persis skripsi
Contoh di bawah dirender dari templat skripsi (studi kasus kemiskinan Jawa Tengah) — format cetaknya sama dengan skripsi sungguhan:






Halaman sampul — judul, “SKRIPSI”, FEB Universitas Diponegoro, nama & NIM







BAB bernomor + Tabel 5.1 otomatis & rujukan silang berwarna





Gambar, peta, dan uji statistik pun tampil dalam tata letak skripsi — semuanya dihitung dari data, bukan ditempel manual:



Gambar 5.1 (peta dengan basemap OSM) + uji asumsi klasik, dalam tata letak skripsi

title: Skripsi dengan Quarto
text:
Yang otomatis diurus Quarto

Halaman muka: sampul, pengesahan, abstrak, kata pengantar.
Penomoran: “BAB I–V”, Tabel 5.1 / Gambar 5.1, persamaan — beserta rujukan silang (klik untuk melompat).
Daftar otomatis: daftar isi, daftar tabel, daftar gambar.
Sitasi & daftar pustaka: gaya APA (atau lain), terhubung Zotero/.bib.
Hasil analisis: tabel deskriptif, grafik, peta, uji asumsi klasik, regresi — langsung dari kode + data (lihat halaman Analisis Data).

title: Skripsi dengan Quarto
text:
Struktur proyek skripsi
skripsi/
├── _quarto.yml          # proyek "book": atur BAB, format PDF/Word
├── index.qmd            # halaman judul / abstrak
├── bab/
│   ├── 01-pendahuluan.qmd      # BAB I
│   ├── 02-tinjauan-pustaka.qmd # BAB II
│   ├── 03-metode.qmd           # BAB III
│   ├── 04-hasil.qmd            # BAB IV/V (analisis + pembahasan)
│   └── 05-penutup.qmd
├── data/                # data Anda (.csv, .geojson)
├── tex/preamble.tex     # gaya LaTeX (margin, font Times, penomoran BAB)
└── referensi.bib        # pustaka (ekspor dari Zotero)

title: Skripsi dengan Quarto
text:
Cara pakai

Ambil templat siap pakai (R atau Python) — lihat Unduh di bawah.
Tulis isi tiap bab di bab/*.qmd (Markdown biasa + blok kode untuk analisis).
Ganti data Anda di data/.
Render → PDF skripsi. (Untuk Word: --to docx.)
Data/variabel berubah? Render ulang — tabel, grafik, peta, angka, dan daftar pustaka otomatis menyesuaikan.







TipGanti format kampus



Aturan margin, jenis font (mis. Times New Roman 12pt), spasi 1,5, dan penomoran diatur di tex/preamble.tex dan _quarto.yml — sesuaikan sekali, berlaku untuk seluruh skripsi.

title: Skripsi dengan Quarto
text:
Unduh & templat

📄 Unduh contoh skripsi (PDF) — 35 halaman, hasil render templat.
📦 Unduh templat siap render (ZIP): Templat R · Templat Python — sudah lengkap dengan bab/, data/, tex/preamble.tex, referensi.bib, dan slide sidang.
🔎 Lihat / kloning sumber templat di repo workshop (publik): https://github.com/firmanhadi21/quarto-workshop/tree/main/templates
📘 Buku lengkap “Quarto untuk Skripsi” (panduan bab demi bab): https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/







CatatanCara memakai ZIP




Unduh & ekstrak. 2. Buka folder di RStudio (klik *.Rproj). 3. Ganti isi data/ & tulis tiap bab di bab/. 4. Tekan Render → PDF skripsi.

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text: 🌐 English version: read this in English

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
Agenda (±60 menit)



Waktu
Kegiatan




⏱️ 5′
0. Cek kesiapan


⏱️ 10′
1. Dokumen pertama (HTML + PDF)


⏱️ 15′
2. Tulisan + kode + data


⏱️ 10′
3. Bukti reproducible (ubah data)


⏱️ 10′
4. Slide dari data yang sama


⏱️ 10′
5. Tanya jawab & langkah lanjut



Unduh data contoh: data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv (dari repositori workshop). Taruh di folder kerja Anda, di dalam subfolder data/.

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 0 · Cek kesiapan (5′)
Buat folder kerja baru, buka di RStudio, lalu di Terminal:
quarto check
Cari (✓) untuk Quarto dan LaTeX. (Bila pakai Python: juga untuk Jupyter.)

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 1 · Dokumen pertama (10′)
Buat halo.qmd:
---
title: "Halo Quarto"
format: html
---

## Selamat datang
Paragraf biasa: **tebal**, *miring*, `kode`. Daftar:

- satu
- dua
Tekan Render (atau quarto render halo.qmd). Lalu ganti format: html → format: pdf, render lagi. Isi sama, dua keluaran. 🎉

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 2 · Tulisan + kode + data (15′)
Inti Quarto: kode dijalankan saat render; hasilnya masuk ke dokumen. Buat analisis.qmd (pilih R atau Python):

RPython


---
title: "Kemiskinan Jawa Tengah"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2)
df <- read.csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 <- df[df$tahun == 2023, ]
```

Rata-rata kemiskinan 2023 = **`r round(mean(df23$kemiskinan), 2)`%**.

```{r}
df |> group_by(tahun) |> summarise(rata = mean(kemiskinan)) |>
  ggplot(aes(tahun, rata)) + geom_line() + geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Tahun", y = "Kemiskinan (%)")
```


---
title: "Kemiskinan Jawa Tengah"
format: html
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = pd.read_csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 = df[df["tahun"] == 2023]
print(f"Rata-rata 2023: {df23['kemiskinan'].mean():.2f}%")
```

```{python}
df.groupby("tahun")["kemiskinan"].mean().plot(marker="o")
plt.xlabel("Tahun"); plt.ylabel("Kemiskinan (%)"); plt.show()
```



Render. Perhatikan: angka di kalimat dan grafik sama-sama dari data — tanpa salin-tempel.

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 3 · Bukti reproducible (10′)
Momen “aha”:

Buka data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv.
Ubah Kota Semarang 2023 dari 4.58 → 24.58. Simpan.
Render ulang analisis.qmd.

Angka rata-rata dan grafik langsung berubah. Satu edit data → seluruh dokumen ikut.






PentingWajib tahu: quarto render, bukan sekadar preview



Pratinjau langsung (live preview di localhost) tidak memantau berkas data. Setelah mengubah data, jalankan quarto render di Terminal lalu buka hasilnya — atau ubah-simpan sebuah .qmd agar pratinjau menyegarkan. (Ini jebakan paling umum.)

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 4 · Slide dari data yang sama (10′)
Tak perlu menulis ulang. Ubah header analisis.qmd agar punya dua format slide:
---
title: "Kemiskinan Jawa Tengah"
format:
  revealjs: default     # slide HTML (layar/web)
  beamer:               # slide PDF (proyektor)
    aspectratio: 169
---
Lalu:
quarto render analisis.qmd --to revealjs   # -> analisis.html
quarto render analisis.qmd --to beamer     # -> analisis.pdf
Judul ## menjadi satu slide; grafik tadi muncul di slide — tetap dari data yang sama.

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 5 · Selesai & langkah lanjut (10′)
Anda baru saja: menulis dokumen, menjalankan analisis di dalamnya, membuktikan reproducibility, dan membuat slide — semua dari satu sumber.
Lanjutkan:

📘 Buku “Quarto untuk Skripsi” (lengkap, BAB I–V, templat siap pakai) — https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/
📦 Templat skripsi (R & Python) + slide sidang — di repositori buku.
📑 Dokumentasi Quarto — https://quarto.org/docs/guide/
🔖 Sitasi via Zotero — https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/citations.html

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
🌐 English version: read this in English
Selamat datang! Workshop ini memperkenalkan Quarto — sistem penerbitan dokumen ilmiah yang menyatukan tulisan + kode + data dalam satu berkas, lalu menghasilkan PDF, HTML, Word, dan slide dari sumber yang sama.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Untuk siapa?


🎓 Mahasiswa (S1–S3)
Menyusun skripsi/tesis/disertasi kuantitatif: olah data, tabel, grafik, sitasi, dan PDF sesuai format kampus — tanpa salin-tempel dari SPSS/Excel ke Word.


🔬 Dosen & Peneliti
Membuat artikel, laporan, dan presentasi yang dapat diproduksi ulang. Data berubah? Cukup render ulang — angka, tabel, grafik, dan slide ikut diperbarui otomatis.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Masalah yang kita pecahkan







Alur lama (Word + SPSS/Excel)
Dengan Quarto




Angka disalin manual → rawan salah ketik
Angka muncul otomatis dari kode


Nomor tabel/gambar kacau saat menyisipkan
Penomoran & daftar isi otomatis


Revisi data = ulang olah + salin-tempel
Ganti data → render ulang sekali


Sitasi & daftar pustaka manual
Sitasi otomatis (mis. APA), via Zotero


Slide dibuat ulang terpisah
Slide dari data yang sama (Beamer/Reveal.js)

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Inti idenya
  berkas .qmd  ──►  Quarto  ──►  PDF · HTML · Word · Slides
 (tulisan +        (jalankan      (keluaran rapi,
  kode + data)      kode, rangkai)  reproducible)
Satu sumber, banyak keluaran. Ubah satu hal di data, dan semua hasil ikut berubah. Itulah reproducibility — dan itu yang akan kita buktikan langsung hari ini.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Mulai

👉 Ikuti tutorial hands-on — langkah demi langkah, bisa langsung Anda praktikkan.
📊 Analisis Data — statistik deskriptif, plotting, regresi linear, dan geostatistik (kode R & Python yang berjalan).
🎓 Skripsi dengan Quarto — PDF skripsi sesuai format kampus (sampul, BAB I–V, penomoran & daftar pustaka otomatis).
🖥️ Unduh slide presentasi (PDF/Beamer)
📘 Buku lengkap “Quarto untuk Skripsi”: https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/







TipYang perlu disiapkan



R + RStudio (RStudio sudah membawa Quarto), TinyTeX untuk PDF, dan opsional Python untuk contoh berbahasa Python. Detail di tutorial.

title: Analisis Data dengan Quarto
text:
🌐 English version: read this in English
Halaman ini menunjukkan analisis nyata — dijalankan langsung dari data — untuk R dan Python. Studi kasus: tingkat kemiskinan 35 kabupaten/kota di Jawa Tengah (data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv). Salin kodenya dan jalankan di komputer Anda.
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(knitr)
df <- read.csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 <- df[df$tahun == 2023, ]

title: Analisis Data dengan Quarto
text:
1. Statistik deskriptif
Ringkasan sebaran variabel: pemusatan (rata-rata, median) dan keragaman (simpangan baku, rentang).

RPython



vars <- c("kemiskinan", "pdrb_kapita", "inflasi", "ipm")
ringkas <- data.frame(
  Variabel = vars,
  Rerata = sapply(df23[vars], mean), Median = sapply(df23[vars], median),
  SB = sapply(df23[vars], sd),
  Min = sapply(df23[vars], min), Maks = sapply(df23[vars], max))
kable(ringkas, digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)




Variabel
Rerata
Median
SB
Min
Maks




kemiskinan
10.37
10.61
3.23
4.58
16.50


pdrb_kapita
41.88
41.05
5.66
31.63
56.23


inflasi
2.69
2.67
0.46
1.84
3.52


ipm
79.39
79.17
1.92
76.20
85.10








import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 = df[df["tahun"] == 2023]
df23[["kemiskinan","pdrb_kapita","inflasi","ipm"]].describe().round(2)

       kemiskinan  pdrb_kapita  inflasi    ipm
count       35.00        35.00    35.00  35.00
mean        10.37        41.88     2.69  79.39
std          3.23         5.66     0.46   1.92
min          4.58        31.63     1.84  76.20
25%          7.95        38.80     2.35  77.87
50%         10.61        41.05     2.67  79.17
75%         11.91        46.02     3.10  80.84
max         16.50        56.23     3.52  85.10

title: Analisis Data dengan Quarto
text:
2. Plotting
Visualisasi membantu memahami sebaran (histogram) dan hubungan (diagram pencar).

RPython



par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
hist(df23$kemiskinan, breaks = 10, col = "#b03a2e", border = "white",
     main = "", xlab = "Kemiskinan (%)", ylab = "Jumlah daerah")
plot(df23$pdrb_kapita, df23$kemiskinan, pch = 19, col = "#2166ac",
     xlab = "PDRB per kapita (juta)", ylab = "Kemiskinan (%)")
abline(lm(kemiskinan ~ pdrb_kapita, data = df23), col = "#b03a2e", lwd = 2)




Sebaran kemiskinan (histogram) & hubungannya dengan PDRB per kapita, 2023







import numpy as np, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(8, 3.2))
ax[0].hist(df23["kemiskinan"], bins=10, color="#b03a2e", edgecolor="white")
ax[0].set_xlabel("Kemiskinan (%)"); ax[0].set_ylabel("Jumlah daerah")
ax[1].scatter(df23["pdrb_kapita"], df23["kemiskinan"], color="#2166ac")
m, b = np.polyfit(df23["pdrb_kapita"], df23["kemiskinan"], 1)
ax[1].plot(df23["pdrb_kapita"], m*df23["pdrb_kapita"] + b, color="#b03a2e")
ax[1].set_xlabel("PDRB per kapita (juta)"); ax[1].set_ylabel("Kemiskinan (%)")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()




Sebaran kemiskinan (histogram) & hubungannya dengan PDRB per kapita, 2023

title: Analisis Data dengan Quarto
text:
3. Regresi linear berganda
Memodelkan kemiskinan sebagai fungsi beberapa variabel bebas. Perhatikan tanda koefisien (arah pengaruh), p-value (signifikansi), dan R² (daya jelas model). Pakai data panel 2019–2023 (175 observasi).

RPython



model <- lm(kemiskinan ~ pdrb_kapita + inflasi + ipm + pengangguran, data = df)
koef <- summary(model)$coefficients[, c("Estimate", "Pr(>|t|)")]
rownames(koef) <- c("Konstanta","PDRB per kapita","Inflasi","IPM","Pengangguran")
kable(koef, col.names = c("Koefisien", "p-value"), digits = 3)





Koefisien
p-value




Konstanta
98.355
0.000


PDRB per kapita
-0.123
0.001


Inflasi
0.198
0.112


IPM
-1.077
0.000


Pengangguran
0.235
0.031




cat(sprintf("R-squared = %.3f", summary(model)$r.squared))

R-squared = 0.533





import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
model = smf.ols("kemiskinan ~ pdrb_kapita + inflasi + ipm + pengangguran", data=df).fit()
koef = pd.DataFrame({"Koefisien": model.params, "p-value": model.pvalues}).round(3)
koef.index = ["Konstanta","PDRB per kapita","Inflasi","IPM","Pengangguran"]
print(koef)

                 Koefisien  p-value
Konstanta           98.355    0.000
PDRB per kapita     -0.123    0.001
Inflasi              0.198    0.112
IPM                 -1.077    0.000
Pengangguran         0.235    0.031

print(f"R-squared = {model.rsquared:.3f}")

R-squared = 0.533





Membaca hasil: tanda koefisien = arah pengaruh (mis. koefisien negatif pada IPM ⇒ makin tinggi IPM, makin rendah kemiskinan); p-value < 0,05 ⇒ pengaruh signifikan; R² = proporsi variasi yang dijelaskan model. R² yang rendah pada model sederhana ini justru wajar — data panel sering butuh efek tetap (fixed effects) atau regresi spasial (lihat buku). Itulah gunanya reproducible: ganti model, render ulang, bandingkan.

title: Analisis Data dengan Quarto
text:
4. Geostatistik
Data spasial punya pesan tambahan: apakah daerah miskin mengelompok? Kita petakan sebarannya, lalu uji autokorelasi spasial dengan Moran’s I.

Peta tematik (choropleth)

RPython



library(sf); library(maptiles); library(tidyterra)
geo <- st_transform(st_read("data/jateng.geojson", quiet = TRUE), 3857)
peta <- merge(geo, df23, by = "wilayah", all.x = TRUE)
latar <- get_tiles(peta, provider = "OpenStreetMap", zoom = 8, crop = TRUE)
ggplot() + geom_spatraster_rgb(data = latar) +
  geom_sf(data = peta, aes(fill = kemiskinan), color = "white", linewidth = 0.1, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "OrRd", direction = 1, name = "Kemiskinan (%)") + theme_void()




Sebaran kemiskinan kabupaten/kota, 2023 (R)







import geopandas as gpd, contextily as ctx
g = gpd.read_file("data/jateng.geojson")
pmap = g.merge(df23, on="wilayah", how="left").to_crs(3857)
ax = pmap.plot(column="kemiskinan", cmap="OrRd", legend=True, alpha=0.85,
               edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.3, figsize=(8, 4.2))
ctx.add_basemap(ax, source=ctx.providers.OpenStreetMap.Mapnik)
ax.set_axis_off(); plt.show()




Sebaran kemiskinan kabupaten/kota, 2023 (Python)









Autokorelasi spasial — Moran’s I
Moran’s I positif & signifikan ⇒ daerah dengan nilai serupa berdekatan (mengelompok).

RPython



library(spdep)
nb <- poly2nb(peta)                 # ketetanggaan (queen contiguity)
w  <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")     # bobot spasial
mt <- moran.test(peta$kemiskinan, w)
cat(sprintf("Moran's I = %.3f (p = %.4f)", mt$estimate[1], mt$p.value))

Moran's I = 0.294 (p = 0.0038)





from libpysal.weights import Queen
from esda.moran import Moran
w = Queen.from_dataframe(pmap, use_index=False); w.transform = "r"
mi = Moran(pmap["kemiskinan"].values, w)
print(f"Moran's I = {mi.I:.3f} (p = {mi.p_sim:.4f})")

Moran's I = 0.294 (p = 0.0110)





Tafsir: bila Moran’s I positif & signifikan, kemiskinan mengelompok secara spasial — ada “kantong” kemiskinan. Lanjutkan dengan LISA (klaster lokal) dan regresi spasial bila perlu (lihat buku, bab Statistik Spasial).

title: Analisis Data dengan Quarto
text:
Selanjutnya

📘 Buku “Quarto untuk Skripsi” — uji asumsi klasik, data panel, LISA, regresi spasial: https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/
🧪 Kembali ke tutorial hands-on.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
🌐 Versi Bahasa Indonesia: baca dalam Bahasa Indonesia
Welcome! This workshop introduces Quarto — a scientific publishing system that unites prose + code + data in a single file and renders to PDF, HTML, Word, and slides from one source.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Who is this for?


🎓 Students
Writing a quantitative thesis/dissertation: data analysis, tables, figures, citations, and a campus-formatted PDF — without copy-pasting from SPSS/Excel into Word.


🔬 Lecturers & Researchers
Producing reproducible papers, reports, and presentations. Data changed? Just re-render — numbers, tables, figures, and slides update automatically.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
The problem we solve







The old way (Word + SPSS/Excel)
With Quarto




Numbers copied by hand → typos
Numbers appear automatically from code


Table/figure numbering breaks on insert
Numbering & table of contents automatic


Revising data = redo analysis + re-paste
Change data → re-render once


Manual citations & bibliography
Automatic citations (e.g. APA), via Zotero


Slides rebuilt separately
Slides from the same data (Beamer/Reveal.js)

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
The core idea
  .qmd file   ──►  Quarto  ──►  PDF · HTML · Word · Slides
 (prose +         (run the      (clean, reproducible
  code + data)     code, weave)   output)
One source, many outputs. Change one thing in the data, and everything updates. That’s reproducibility — and we’ll prove it live today.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Get started

👉 Follow the hands-on tutorial — step by step, do it yourself.
📊 Data Analysis — descriptive statistics, plotting, linear regression, and geostatistics (live R & Python code).
🎓 A Thesis with Quarto — a thesis PDF in campus format (cover, Chapters I–V, automatic numbering & bibliography).
🖥️ Download the slides (PDF/Beamer)
📘 Full book “Quarto untuk Skripsi” (Indonesian): https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/







TipWhat to install



R + RStudio (RStudio ships with Quarto), TinyTeX for PDF, and optionally Python for the Python examples. Details in the tutorial.

title: Data Analysis with Quarto
text:
🌐 Versi Bahasa Indonesia: baca dalam Bahasa Indonesia
This page shows real analysis — run straight from the data — in both R and Python. Case study: poverty across 35 districts of Central Java (data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv). Copy the code and run it on your machine.
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(knitr)
df <- read.csv("../data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 <- df[df$tahun == 2023, ]

title: Data Analysis with Quarto
text:
1. Descriptive statistics
Summaries of each variable: central tendency (mean, median) and spread (standard deviation, range).

RPython



vars <- c("kemiskinan", "pdrb_kapita", "inflasi", "ipm")
ringkas <- data.frame(
  Variable = vars,
  Mean = sapply(df23[vars], mean), Median = sapply(df23[vars], median),
  SD = sapply(df23[vars], sd),
  Min = sapply(df23[vars], min), Max = sapply(df23[vars], max))
kable(ringkas, digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)




Variable
Mean
Median
SD
Min
Max




kemiskinan
10.37
10.61
3.23
4.58
16.50


pdrb_kapita
41.88
41.05
5.66
31.63
56.23


inflasi
2.69
2.67
0.46
1.84
3.52


ipm
79.39
79.17
1.92
76.20
85.10








import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv("../data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 = df[df["tahun"] == 2023]
df23[["kemiskinan","pdrb_kapita","inflasi","ipm"]].describe().round(2)

       kemiskinan  pdrb_kapita  inflasi    ipm
count       35.00        35.00    35.00  35.00
mean        10.37        41.88     2.69  79.39
std          3.23         5.66     0.46   1.92
min          4.58        31.63     1.84  76.20
25%          7.95        38.80     2.35  77.87
50%         10.61        41.05     2.67  79.17
75%         11.91        46.02     3.10  80.84
max         16.50        56.23     3.52  85.10

title: Data Analysis with Quarto
text:
2. Plotting
Visualization reveals the distribution (histogram) and relationships (scatter).

RPython



par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
hist(df23$kemiskinan, breaks = 10, col = "#b03a2e", border = "white",
     main = "", xlab = "Poverty (%)", ylab = "Count")
plot(df23$pdrb_kapita, df23$kemiskinan, pch = 19, col = "#2166ac",
     xlab = "GDP per capita", ylab = "Poverty (%)")
abline(lm(kemiskinan ~ pdrb_kapita, data = df23), col = "#b03a2e", lwd = 2)




Poverty distribution (histogram) & its relation to GDP per capita, 2023







import numpy as np, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(8, 3.2))
ax[0].hist(df23["kemiskinan"], bins=10, color="#b03a2e", edgecolor="white")
ax[0].set_xlabel("Poverty (%)"); ax[0].set_ylabel("Count")
ax[1].scatter(df23["pdrb_kapita"], df23["kemiskinan"], color="#2166ac")
m, b = np.polyfit(df23["pdrb_kapita"], df23["kemiskinan"], 1)
ax[1].plot(df23["pdrb_kapita"], m*df23["pdrb_kapita"] + b, color="#b03a2e")
ax[1].set_xlabel("GDP per capita"); ax[1].set_ylabel("Poverty (%)")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()




Poverty distribution (histogram) & its relation to GDP per capita, 2023

title: Data Analysis with Quarto
text:
3. Multiple linear regression
Model poverty as a function of several predictors. Watch the sign of each coefficient (direction), the p-value (significance), and R² (explained variance). We use the 2019–2023 panel (175 observations).

RPython



model <- lm(kemiskinan ~ pdrb_kapita + inflasi + ipm + pengangguran, data = df)
koef <- summary(model)$coefficients[, c("Estimate", "Pr(>|t|)")]
rownames(koef) <- c("Intercept","GDP per capita","Inflation","HDI","Unemployment")
kable(koef, col.names = c("Coefficient", "p-value"), digits = 3)





Coefficient
p-value




Intercept
98.355
0.000


GDP per capita
-0.123
0.001


Inflation
0.198
0.112


HDI
-1.077
0.000


Unemployment
0.235
0.031




cat(sprintf("R-squared = %.3f", summary(model)$r.squared))

R-squared = 0.533





import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
model = smf.ols("kemiskinan ~ pdrb_kapita + inflasi + ipm + pengangguran", data=df).fit()
koef = pd.DataFrame({"Coefficient": model.params, "p-value": model.pvalues}).round(3)
koef.index = ["Intercept","GDP per capita","Inflation","HDI","Unemployment"]
print(koef)

                Coefficient  p-value
Intercept            98.355    0.000
GDP per capita       -0.123    0.001
Inflation             0.198    0.112
HDI                  -1.077    0.000
Unemployment          0.235    0.031

print(f"R-squared = {model.rsquared:.3f}")

R-squared = 0.533





Reading it: a coefficient’s sign is the direction (e.g. a negative HDI coefficient ⇒ higher HDI, lower poverty); p-value < 0.05 ⇒ significant; R² = share of variance explained. A low R² for this simple model is expected — panel data often needs fixed effects or spatial regression (see the book). That’s the point of being reproducible: swap the model, re-render, compare.

title: Data Analysis with Quarto
text:
4. Geostatistics
Spatial data carries an extra message: do poor districts cluster? We map the distribution, then test for spatial autocorrelation with Moran’s I.

Choropleth map

RPython



library(sf); library(maptiles); library(tidyterra)
geo <- st_transform(st_read("../data/jateng.geojson", quiet = TRUE), 3857)
peta <- merge(geo, df23, by = "wilayah", all.x = TRUE)
latar <- get_tiles(peta, provider = "OpenStreetMap", zoom = 8, crop = TRUE)
ggplot() + geom_spatraster_rgb(data = latar) +
  geom_sf(data = peta, aes(fill = kemiskinan), color = "white", linewidth = 0.1, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "OrRd", direction = 1, name = "Poverty (%)") + theme_void()




Poverty by district, 2023 (R)







import geopandas as gpd, contextily as ctx
g = gpd.read_file("../data/jateng.geojson")
pmap = g.merge(df23, on="wilayah", how="left").to_crs(3857)
ax = pmap.plot(column="kemiskinan", cmap="OrRd", legend=True, alpha=0.85,
               edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.3, figsize=(8, 4.2))
ctx.add_basemap(ax, source=ctx.providers.OpenStreetMap.Mapnik)
ax.set_axis_off(); plt.show()




Poverty by district, 2023 (Python)









Spatial autocorrelation — Moran’s I
A positive, significant Moran’s I ⇒ similar values sit next to each other (clustering).

RPython



library(spdep)
nb <- poly2nb(peta)                 # neighbours (queen contiguity)
w  <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")     # spatial weights
mt <- moran.test(peta$kemiskinan, w)
cat(sprintf("Moran's I = %.3f (p = %.4f)", mt$estimate[1], mt$p.value))

Moran's I = 0.294 (p = 0.0038)





from libpysal.weights import Queen
from esda.moran import Moran
w = Queen.from_dataframe(pmap, use_index=False); w.transform = "r"
mi = Moran(pmap["kemiskinan"].values, w)
print(f"Moran's I = {mi.I:.3f} (p = {mi.p_sim:.4f})")

Moran's I = 0.294 (p = 0.0080)





Interpretation: if Moran’s I is positive and significant, poverty clusters spatially — there are poverty “pockets.” Follow up with LISA (local clusters) and spatial regression if needed (see the book’s Spatial Statistics chapter).

title: Data Analysis with Quarto
text:
Next steps

📘 Book “Quarto untuk Skripsi” — classical assumption tests, panel data, LISA, spatial regression (Indonesian): https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/
🧪 Back to the hands-on tutorial.
